title: Cymatic Planner PASS: BFS with first-class minimality witness (frontier exhaustion at depths 0 and 1)
_fixture_purpose: Demonstrates minimality_witness as a first-class certificate claim. BFS from flat to hexagons with max_depth_k=3: depth-0 frontier = {flat} (no hexagons reachable in 0 steps), depth-1 frontier = {stripes} (no hexagons reachable in 1 step), hexagons found at depth 2. The minimality_witness records all three invariants: proved_no_path_shorter_than=2, frontier_sizes=[1,1], excluded_shorter_lengths=[0,1]. The cert says 'all shorter depths were exhausted' — shortness is part of the witness, not just a validator-side inference. QA analogue: proving that the orbit distance from singularity to cosmos is exactly 2 by exhibiting the BFS frontier at each depth and showing the cosmos orbit cannot be reached in fewer than 2 generator applications.

qa_mapping.planning_qa_analogue: Minimality witness carries the BFS frontier exhaustion evidence as a first-class cert claim. Depth 0: only 'flat' (singularity orbit) — no cosmos state reachable in 0 steps. Depth 1: only 'stripes' (satellite orbit) — satellite is the mandatory threshold, cosmos not reachable in 1 step. Depth 2: 'hexagons' found. The orbit-distance from singularity to cosmos is provably 2, with satellite as the only intermediate — consistent with the QA invariant that the norm threshold separating orbit families cannot be crossed in a single generator step from the fixed point.

validation_checks.details: bfs in {bfs, dfs, astar, dijkstra, iddfs}

validation_checks.description: every plan step is a legal edge in control_graph
observed final pattern class matches target pattern class
no disordered/out_of_orbit state visited in plan
validation_checks.details: plan visits only flat, stripes, hexagons

validation_checks.description: minimality_witness internally consistent and consistent with path_length_k
validation_checks.details: proved_no_path_shorter_than=2 == path_length_k=2; excluded_shorter_lengths=[0,1] == range(2); len(frontier_sizes)=2 == path_length_k=2
